ground_truth.title: Should You Invest in a Sewer Camera?
ground_truth.author: PlumbingZone
ground_truth.main_content: Should You Invest in a Sewer Camera? - A discussion on Plumbing Zone forum. Customers are often shocked at the price of diagnosis and repairs for plumbing. While not every plumber needs a sewer camera, it will definitely give you an advantage in certain markets. If you work in old neighborhoods or get called to diagnose clogs, backups, or odors in homes, you will probably see your business benefit if you invest in a sewer camera. Advantages include: gaining customer trust by showing them the actual problem, accurate diagnosis of clogs and pipe damage, and cost savings compared to renting. Forum replies share mixed experiences - some plumbers report that customers rarely want to pay for camera inspection, while others recommend using it as a tool to sell repairs rather than charging separately for the inspection.
with: Customers are often shocked at the price of diagnosis and repairs for plumbing.
While not every plumber needs one, it will definitely give you an advantage in certain markets.
A sewer camera is one of the best tools for finding out the cause and location of a clog.
Just like a customer doesn't expect the doctor to tell them what's wrong at no charge, they shouldn't expect you to tell them why their basement smells like sewage without expecting to pay for it.
Time to start using it as a tool to sell a job instead of trying to charge extra for camera use.
